name: UL 4600: Standard for Safety for the Evaluation of Autonomous Products
disclaimer: Informative only. Not a substitute for reading the published standard.

entries.title: Safety case approach
entries.summary: Establishes the requirement for a structured safety case as the primary mechanism for demonstrating autonomous product safety.

entries.title: Safety case framework
entries.summary: Requires construction of a goal-structured safety case using claims, arguments, and evidence to demonstrate safety of the autonomous product.

entries.title: Safety case completeness
entries.summary: Mandates that the safety case addresses all identified hazards, operational domains, and lifecycle phases of the autonomous product.

entries.title: Safety case maintenance
entries.summary: Requires ongoing maintenance of the safety case to reflect design changes, new evidence, and field experience throughout the product lifecycle.

entries.title: Risk assessment
entries.summary: Covers systematic identification and evaluation of risks associated with autonomous product operation.

entries.title: Hazard identification
entries.summary: Requires systematic identification of hazards relevant to the autonomous product including novel hazards from autonomy.

entries.title: Risk evaluation and prioritization
entries.summary: Defines methods for evaluating identified risks by severity and likelihood, and establishing priority for risk mitigation.

entries.title: Acceptable risk criteria
entries.summary: Requires definition of risk acceptance criteria appropriate to the application domain and severity of potential harm.

entries.title: Interactions and dependencies
entries.summary: Addresses safety considerations for interactions between the autonomous system, humans, infrastructure, and other systems.

entries.title: Human interaction safety
entries.summary: Specifies requirements for safe interaction between the autonomous product and human users, operators, and bystanders.

entries.title: Environmental interaction
entries.summary: Addresses safety in the context of the autonomous system operating within its physical environment, including edge cases.

entries.title: Autonomy and decision-making
entries.summary: Covers safety requirements for the autonomous decision-making capabilities of the product.

entries.title: Autonomy level definition
entries.summary: Requires clear definition and documentation of the autonomy level at which the product operates and the boundaries of its autonomous behavior.

entries.title: Operational design domain
entries.summary: Mandates specification of the operational design domain including geographic, environmental, temporal, and traffic conditions under which the product operates.

entries.title: Minimal risk condition
entries.summary: Requires the system to achieve a minimal risk condition when it can no longer safely perform its intended function.

entries.title: Sensing, perception, and planning
entries.summary: Addresses safety of the perception pipeline, world modeling, and planning algorithms used by the autonomous product.

entries.title: Sensor sufficiency
entries.summary: Requires demonstration that the sensor suite is sufficient for safe operation within the declared operational design domain.

entries.title: Perception validation
entries.summary: Specifies validation requirements for the perception system including object detection, classification, and tracking accuracy.

entries.title: Data integrity
entries.summary: Covers requirements for data quality, integrity, and management throughout the autonomous product lifecycle.

entries.title: Training data quality
entries.summary: Requires documented processes to ensure training data quality, including representativeness, accuracy, and absence of harmful biases.

entries.title: Data provenance and traceability
entries.summary: Mandates traceability of data used for training, validation, and testing back to its source and processing history.

entries.title: Data security
entries.summary: Addresses protection of data against unauthorized modification, corruption, or access that could compromise safety.

entries.title: Verification and validation
entries.summary: Covers V&V activities specific to autonomous products including testing, analysis, and simulation approaches.

entries.title: V&V strategy for autonomous systems
entries.summary: Requires a comprehensive V&V strategy that addresses the challenges unique to autonomous system verification such as combinatorial complexity.

entries.title: Testing adequacy
entries.summary: Specifies requirements for demonstrating test adequacy including coverage metrics, scenario diversity, and edge case inclusion.

entries.title: Simulation in V&V
entries.summary: Addresses the role of simulation in verification and validation, including simulation fidelity requirements and gap analysis.

entries.title: Machine learning model V&V
entries.summary: Specifies V&V requirements unique to machine learning components including robustness testing, performance characterization, and bias evaluation.

entries.title: Tool qualification and process assurance
entries.summary: Addresses qualification of tools used in the development and V&V of autonomous products.

entries.title: Development tool qualification
entries.summary: Requires qualification of development tools that may impact the safety of the autonomous product, including ML training frameworks.

entries.title: Lifecycle and post-deployment
entries.summary: Covers activities during and after deployment including monitoring, maintenance, and decommissioning of autonomous products.

entries.title: Field performance monitoring
entries.summary: Requires continuous monitoring of field performance with mechanisms to detect safety-relevant anomalies and degradation.

entries.title: Software and model updates
entries.summary: Addresses safe deployment of software and model updates including change impact analysis, regression testing, and rollback capability.
